title: Устройство объекта в куче: заголовок, объект-тип, таблица методов

cards.prompt: All instances of a type share ONE type object: their type-object pointers point at the same type object and GetType returns that pointer, so ReferenceEquals(a.GetType(), b.GetType()) is True
cards.code: using System; var a = new object(); var b = new object(); Console.WriteLine(object.ReferenceEquals(a.GetType(), b.GetType()));

cards.prompt: GetType is non-virtual and reads the type-object pointer, so it cannot lie: two Managers share the Manager type object (True) while Manager and Employee are different type objects (False). Prints True False
cards.code: using System; class Employee{} class Manager:Employee{} var m1=new Manager(); var m2=new Manager(); var e=new Employee(); Console.WriteLine($"{object.ReferenceEquals(m1.GetType(),m2.GetType())} {object.ReferenceEquals(m1.GetType(),e.GetType())}");

cards.prompt: An object header is not free: even a field-less class instance is 24 bytes on 64-bit — 8 (sync-block index) + 8 (type-object pointer) + 8 (minimum size/alignment). The GC-allocation delta around one new Empty() prints 24
cards.code: using System; class Empty{} var w=new Empty(); long a=GC.GetAllocatedBytesForCurrentThread(); var x=new Empty(); long b=GC.GetAllocatedBytesForCurrentThread(); Console.WriteLine(b-a);
